channel.title: PC Workman — Build in Public Blog
channel.description: Three weekly series documenting the development of PC Workman, an AI-powered Windows system monitor: Monday Grind Blueprint, Wednesday Code Autopsy, Friday Shipped & Scarred. By Marcin Firmuga, HCK_Labs.

item.title: I Was Supposed to Fix Bugs. I Accidentally Built Two Features Instead. PC Workman 1.7.8
item.description: Thermal baseline learning and voltage SPC — the two features that grew out of a bug-fixing week. PC Workman 1.7.8.

item.title: I Built an AI-Powered PC Monitor in Python. 28 Strangers Shaped Its Brain. PC Workman 1.7.6
item.description: How 28 community members shaped hck_GPT's 82 intents. DeepMonitor, the 2.5D PC map and Wave 2 of the offline assistant.

item.title: Wednesday Code Autopsy #4: The Canvas Arc Nobody Uses — PC Workman
item.description: Tkinter's create_arc — the forgotten canvas primitive behind PC Workman's driver health gauge.

item.title: Monday Grind Blueprint #3: I Built a Driver Booster Competitor From Scratch — PC Workman v1.7.2
item.description: The week's plan: driver health scanning without the bloatware — what became First Setup & Drivers.

item.title: Friday Shipped & Scarred #3: PC Workman 1.7.1 — The Foundation Great Release
item.description: Stats Engine v2 shipped: the SQLite time-series pipeline every later feature stands on.

item.title: Monday Grind Blueprint #2: 80 Processes Explained, 3 Issues Targeted, 1 Week to Ship
item.description: Growing the process library and committing publicly to three community-reported issues in one week.

item.title: Friday Shipped & Scarred #1: The Day git --force Deleted 130 PC Workman Commits
item.description: The week the repo died on a Tuesday. Recovery via reflog — 90 of 130 commits back — and the backup rule born from it.

item.title: Code Autopsy #1: How ~90 Lines Turned System Monitoring Into A Conversation
item.description: The first intent parser — the ~90-line seed that grew into hck_GPT's 82 offline intents.
